title: How to File a Labour/Employment Complaint
description: Step-by-step guide for workplace disputes including unpaid wages, wrongful termination, and harassment

steps.details: Collect evidence: salary slips, appointment letter, termination letter, messages, emails, attendance records, witness contacts. Document dates and details of the violation.

steps.action: Send a written complaint to employer
steps.details: Write a formal complaint to your employer/HR department detailing the violation. Send by email and registered post for proof. Give 15 days to respond.
Visit your District Labour Commissioner office or file online on the Shram Suvidha Portal (shramsuvidha.gov.in). Provide all evidence and details.
The Labour Commissioner will call both parties for conciliation. Attend with your documents. Many disputes are resolved at this stage.

steps.action: If unresolved — file case in Labour Court
steps.details: If conciliation fails, the Labour Commissioner will refer the dispute to the Industrial Tribunal/Labour Court. You can also directly approach the court.

steps.action: For sexual harassment — Internal Complaints Committee
steps.details: File complaint with the Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) of your workplace under the POSH Act. Employers with 10+ employees MUST have an ICC. If no ICC exists, approach the Local Complaints Committee.

tips: Always keep a copy of your appointment letter and salary slips
Unpaid salary must be paid within 7-10 days of the due date
Minimum wages are set by the government — check current rates
Wrongful termination without notice period entitles you to compensation
PF contributions are mandatory — check your UAN status
Workplace sexual harassment complaints must be resolved within 90 days
